codeReview.summary: The fix improves ArcPanel by consuming `arc.storyIds`, so entity-derived geopolitical arcs no longer collapse to the old countries-array lookup. However, the upstream provenance is still incomplete: `buildCountryCoOccurrences()` records only one representative event ID for each entity-derived country pair, so the shared-intel drilldown can still omit the other country's contributing story.

issues.description: In the entity-derived branch, `buildCountryCoOccurrences()` collapses each country pair down to a single `eventId` (`byCountry[isos[i]][0]?.eventId || byCountry[isos[j]][0]?.eventId`) before calling `addPair()`. As a result, `arc.storyIds` does not actually contain the full set of events that contributed to the relationship. For a simple FR↔US entity-derived pair, the current code emits `storyIds: ["e1"]` instead of both `e1` and `e2`, so ArcPanel's "SHARED INTEL" section still presents incomplete evidence for the geopolitical connection.
issues.suggestedFix: When building entity-derived pairs, add every contributing event ID from both countries into the pair's `storyIds` collection (not just one representative ID), then keep ArcPanel's storyIds-based drilldown so entity-derived arcs show the complete supporting story set.

summary: Fail. ArcPanel now uses storyIds, but entity-derived arcs still carry incomplete provenance because the pair builder saves only one side's event ID.
